controlling_authority.adoption_scope: design contract only; no grammar, parser, checker, MIR, runtime, tooling, publication, or product activation

surface_decisions.associated_subset: MemberVisibility? type Identifier = ExactVariantUnion; canonical example: +type Weekend = Sat | Sun

Ord<T>.canonical_signature: public trait Ord<T> { +def compare.(borrow lhs: T, borrow rhs: T) -> Int throws Never effects {}; }

Display.canonical_signature: public trait Display { +def display.() -> String throws Never effects {}; }

rules.title: accepted design remains nonactivatable
rules.contract: All three facilities are canonical PREVIEW_DESIGN decisions with no current source, grammar, implementation, publication, promotion, P1 closure, or product-lane effect.

rules.title: order role is owner-specific and singular
rules.contract: A future Enum may select exactly one of enum#increasing or enum#decreasing; both words remain ordinary identifiers outside the exact Enum introducer role.

rules.title: semantic order is representation-independent
rules.contract: SemanticOrderRank and the ordered VariantId vector determine declaration order but are never VariantId numeric value, raw value, tag, discriminant, serialization, layout, ABI, match priority, range, iteration, or transition authority.

rules.title: minimum Ord synthesis profile is closed
rules.contract: Only a nominal nonempty payload-free nongeneric Enum is eligible; compare borrows, is pure, synchronous, throws Never, consumes nothing, observes sign only, and returns zero exactly for the same EnumId and VariantId.

rules.title: Ord synthesis has one evidence origin
rules.contract: The marker forms exactly one whole-Enum Ord witness and zero case witnesses; an explicit same-ground conformance conflicts and no priority, specialization, provider lookup, fallback, or operator glyph activation exists.

rules.title: order evolution has independent residue
rules.contract: Marker, direction, case order, and case-universe changes update ordered-vector and behavior digests while stable VariantIds remain stable; incompatible summaries reject at import or link validation.

rules.title: display mapping is all-or-none and enum-owned
rules.contract: The future ~> token belongs only to Enum case mapping, requires exactly one restricted String template for every inhabitable case once selected, permits duplicate display strings, and supplies no case-name fallback.

rules.title: display template has a bounded borrowed scope
rules.contract: Only named payload binders and admitted read-only paths rooted in them are visible; each hole requires one preselected Display witness and cannot move, mutate, throw, suspend, spawn, escape, or perform hidden provider or locale lookup.

rules.title: display synthesis has one evidence origin
rules.contract: A complete mapping forms exactly one whole-Enum Display witness and zero case witnesses; an explicit same-ground conformance conflicts and runtime witness lookup and source-order fallback are zero.

rules.title: display is behavior not identity
rules.contract: Display text is not raw, serialization, parsing, localization, redaction, ABI, layout, or reverse-map identity; a template mutation changes only Display behavior residue.

rules.title: exact variant is a finite owner identity
rules.contract: The minimum exact-variant type is payload-free (EnumId, VariantId), disjoint from sibling variants, independent of declaration order and representation, and never an open runtime subtype test.

rules.title: associated subset alias is explicit
rules.contract: A future Enum member uses MemberVisibility? type Name = ExactVariantUnion; bare assignment is rejected and case/type namespace collision is deterministic.

rules.title: subset normalization is finite and same-owner
rules.contract: VariantSubsetType contains one EnumId, a deduplicated order-insensitive allowed VariantId set, and a frozen enum-universe digest; cross-owner unions reject. If the allowed set equals that complete frozen universe, the canonical type is the nominal owner Enum and the alias spelling has no independent identity.

rules.title: widening and narrowing are asymmetric
rules.contract: Exact variants and subsets widen to their owner through VariantOwnerWidening; subset inclusion permits subset widening, while owner-to-subset and unrelated subset narrowing require an explicit checked boundary.

rules.title: subset patterns use exact finite coverage
rules.contract: An admitted subset pattern uses its frozen allowed set for usefulness, exhaustiveness, guards, and Phi facts; it does not invoke general subtype search or alter the closed-Union alternative judgment.

rules.title: aliases reuse owner representation and evidence
rules.contract: A subset creates no wrapper, allocation, storage, case, VariantId, raw mapping, layout, or Trait witness; every operation reuses the owner Enum's one nominal witness after the widening proof.

rules.title: subset evolution is explicit and lane-local
rules.contract: Adding a case does not expand an explicit subset and may invalidate a prior full-universe-to-owner normalization until the frozen universe is revalidated; removing an included case or changing its identity breaks the alias residue; membership order is irrelevant and incompatible universe digests reject.

rules.title: deferred profiles imply nothing
rules.contract: Payload ordering, payload-bearing exact variants, generic conditional synthesis, reverse parsing, subset ranges or iteration, bundled Traits, AUTO/VIA, specialization, child-local witness replacement, and product support remain inactive or deferred.
